_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**Waste Classification and Documentation System.** The workspace must build or confirm an internal waste classification process that meets 30 TAC Chapter 335 Subchapter R. Generators must classify wastes at the point of generation and retain documentation describing the rationale for each waste classification, including analytical data and process knowledge. Generators must complete TCEQ Form 20957 (Industrial Waste Classification Audit Check Sheet) as part of any TCEQ waste audit. Gap from baseline: most freight operations lack a formalized waste stream mapping tool for cargo-incident residues (e.g., damaged lithium battery packs, spilled cargo chemicals, film-set chemical waste, art conservation materials). Scope: all Texas warehouse and transshipment points. No specific deadline, but TCEQ randomly audits waste stream notifications annually, so readiness must be continuous.

**E-Manifest Infrastructure.** E-manifests are the preferred method to manifest hazardous waste shipments. The workspace should onboard to the EPA e-manifest system (RCRAInfo) for all Texas hazardous waste movements and ensure transporter partner contracts require e-manifest compliance. Gap from baseline: road freight vendors handling cargo residues may still use paper manifests; contract clauses requiring e-manifest use should be added at next renewal. Scope: all Texas-originating hazardous waste shipments.

**Fleet Emissions Inspection Scheduling.** For vehicle fleets registered in I/M program counties (DFW, HGB, ARR, El Paso, and Bexar County effective November 1, 2026), the workspace must implement a compliance calendar that tracks registration renewal dates and OBD inspection deadlines per vehicle. The workspace must also account for the extended three-year initial inspection exemption for rental vehicles under SB 2102 (effective September 1, 2023; being codified in 30 TAC Chapter 114 via Rule Project No. 2026-003-114-AI). Gap from baseline: mixed owned/leased/rented fleets require differentiated scheduling logic. Scope: all Texas ground transport assets in I/M counties.

**Air Permit Compliance Tracking.** If the workspace holds any air authorization under a TCEQ General Operating Permit (GOP), it must track renewal dates and promptly apply for a new Authorization to Operate (ATO) when a GOP is renewed. ACI GOP No. 518 was renewed effective April 30, 2026, requiring current permit holders to submit a new ATO application no later than 90 days from renewal (by approximately July 29, 2026) if any emission units, applicability determinations, or authorization bases have changed. Gap from baseline: air permit renewal tracking is rarely integrated into freight operations management systems. Scope: any Texas fixed facility with a combustion unit, generator, or other permitted emission source.

**Supplier/Vendor Contract Clauses.** Contracts with Texas-based ground carriers, warehouse operators, and waste disposal vendors should be reviewed to include representations of TCEQ registration status, waste classification compliance, and e-manifest capability. Training for operations staff on TCEQ waste classification procedures and I/M fleet compliance calendars is recommended.

*Source: How to Classify Industrial and Hazardous Waste, TCEQ, March 2026. https://www.tceq.texas.gov/permitting/waste_permits/ihw_permits/HW_Am_I_Regulated.html; Industrial and Hazardous Waste Reporting, TCEQ, March 2026. https://www.tceq.texas.gov/permitting/registration/ihw/waste_reporting.html; Air Permitting Rules and Rulemaking, TCEQ, February 2026. https://www.tceq.texas.gov/permitting/air/rules*
